# LLM Inference Hardware Calculator

> Interactive calculator for estimating the GPU hardware needed to host large
> language models for inference.

Canonical URL: https://dipenbhuva.github.io/llm-inference-hardware-calculator/

## What This Site Does

- Estimates model weight memory from parameter count and quantization.
- Calculates KV cache memory from layers, KV heads, head dimension, context
  length, concurrency, and KV cache dtype.
- Estimates GPU VRAM and system RAM requirements.
- Checks vLLM-style serving capacity, tensor parallel fit, and concurrency.
- Provides OOM diagnostics and a starter `vllm serve` command.

## Useful For

- AI accelerator students learning production-ready LLM inference.
- Engineers comparing GPU choices before hosting an LLM.
- Labs about VRAM sizing, KV cache math, tensor parallelism, replicas, and
  serving capacity.

## Important Context

Model templates are reference architecture profiles, not exact public model
names. For production sizing, compare the calculator fields with the target
model configuration from the model provider or checkpoint repository.

## Repository

GitHub: https://github.com/dipenbhuva/llm-inference-hardware-calculator
